message.content: Adversaries may use third-party libraries to compress or encrypt data before exfiltration, enhancing stealth and efficiency. Common libraries include Python's rarfile, libzip, and zlib, with some preinstalled on systems like bzip2 on macOS/Linux and zip on Windows. Unlike utilities, these libraries can be linked during compilation, avoiding the need for subshell execution. Notable examples include BADFLICK using aPLib, BBSRAT and Cardinal RAT using ZLIB, and Denis, Epic, and SeaDuke employing zlib for data compression. Groups like Lazarus and Threat Group-3390 have used these methods to secure data before exfiltration. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring for unusual library usage, implementing robust data loss prevention (DLP) solutions, and employing network monitoring to detect abnormal data compression and encryption activities.
